skill.name: docs-review
skill.description: Review Metabase documentation changes for writing style, structure, clarity, terminology, and pull request feedback format.
skill.summary: Review Metabase documentation changes against the project writing style guide.
skill.author: metabase

security_audit.summary: Static shell, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives from Markdown text, style-guide wording, and MCP tool names. The skill is not malicious, but it allows Bash and can post GitHub pull request review comments when MCP tools are available, so publication should include a side-effect warning.

medium_findings.title: Permitted Bash and GitHub Review Side Effects
medium_findings.description: The skill metadata permits Bash, and the PR workflow instructs the assistant to create, comment on, and submit a GitHub pending review when MCP tools are available. This matches the documentation-review purpose, but it can create external side effects and should be disclosed to marketplace users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly allow Bash and describe GitHub PR review actions. The behavior appears legitimate for the skill, but it is an external side-effect risk.

low_findings.title: Shell Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick alerts point to inline code formatting, fenced Markdown examples, and MCP tool names. No executable Ruby code, command construction, shell interpolation, or user-controlled command execution was found in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text is documentation and Markdown formatting, not source code. The file contains no command execution implementation at these locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text-Matching False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts point to ordinary words in style guidance and example review text. No hashing, encryption, cryptographic API, credential handling, or security-sensitive algorithm usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language documentation rules and sample issue text. There is no cryptographic behavior to exploit.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance alerts point to phrases inside writing guidance, such as avoiding weak phrasing and clutter. No host enumeration, network scanning, or data collection instruction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are part of a writing checklist and PR review formatting instructions. They do not direct system or network discovery.

content.value_statement: Documentation reviews often miss style, structure, and terminology issues. This skill applies the Metabase writing guide and produces focused, numbered feedback.
actual_capabilities: Reviews documentation diffs and files against the Metabase writing style guide.
Checks tone, clarity, headings, terminology, formatting, links, and examples.
Numbers every issue sequentially so authors can reference specific feedback.
Supports local review output when GitHub MCP review tools are unavailable.
Supports GitHub pending review workflows when the required MCP tools are available.
limitations: Designed for Metabase documentation standards, not a general grammar checker.
Does not verify product behavior unless supporting files or context are provided.
May post GitHub review comments when PR review tools are available.
Requires human judgment for subjective style and audience decisions.

use_cases.title: Review a documentation pull request
use_cases.description: Check changed Markdown files for style-guide issues before merging a Metabase documentation update.

use_cases.title: Improve draft developer docs
use_cases.description: Get focused feedback on clarity, instructions, headings, examples, and terminology before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Apply consistent editorial standards
use_cases.description: Find wording, structure, and formatting issues that conflict with established Metabase documentation guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Review a small docs change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docs-review skill to review this Markdown file for Metabase writing style issues. Return only numbered issues worth fixing.

prompt_templates.title: Check a documentation diff
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docs-review skill to review the changed documentation lines in this diff. Focus on clarity, headings, terminology, and examples.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare PR feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docs-review skill on this documentation pull request. If GitHub review tools are available, use the pending review workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a docs section for consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docs-review skill to compare these related documentation files. Identify style, terminology, and structure issues that affect consistency.

output_examples.input: A Markdown draft uses formal wording, vague headings, and a link labeled here.
output: Issue 1: Formal tone. The sentence could be more conversational and direct.
Issue 2: Vague heading. The heading should state the action or outcome.
Issue 3: Unclear link text. Replace here with descriptive link text.

output_examples.input: A pull request changes setup instructions and includes commands out of order.
output: Issue 1: Commands out of order. Put the commands in the order readers should run them.
Issue 2: Missing context. Add a short explanation before the command sequence.

best_practices: Provide the diff or changed files so feedback can reference the right lines.
Ask for issues worth fixing, not a full rewrite of every sentence.
Review the suggested feedback before publishing comments to a pull request.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for technical accuracy review.
Do not ask it to rewrite unrelated product copy outside Metabase style guidance.
Do not enable GitHub review submission without checking the comments first.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews documentation changes for Metabase style, tone, clarity, formatting, links, headings, and examples.

faq.question: Can it review pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. When GitHub MCP review tools are available, it can use a pending review workflow for PR comments.

faq.question: Does it change files automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill is written to provide review feedback, not to edit documentation files directly.

faq.question: Is it only for Metabase documentation?
faq.answer: It is tailored to Metabase writing standards. Other projects can use it, but some advice may not fit.

faq.question: Why are issues numbered?
faq.answer: Numbered issues help authors discuss, track, and fix specific feedback items without confusion.

faq.question: What should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide the changed Markdown, a pull request diff, or the documentation files you want reviewed.
